info.title: InterServer Management API
info.description:
# Overview

The InterServer Management API provides programmatic access to manage your InterServer services. Use this REST API to automate provisioning, configuration, and billing operations across your account.

The API covers the following service categories:
- [Domains](https://www.interserver.net/domains/) — registration, transfers, and DNS management
- [Web Hosting](https://www.interserver.net/hosting/) — shared and reseller hosting
- [VPS Hosting](https://www.interserver.net/vps/) — virtual private servers
- [Dedicated Servers](https://www.interserver.net/dedicated/) — bare metal and [Rapid Deploy Servers](https://www.interserver.net/dedicated/rapid-deploy.html)
- [Backups](https://www.interserver.net/storage/) — storage and backup services
- Licenses — control panel and software licenses
- [Mail](https://www.mail.baby/) — mail delivery services
- SSL — certificate provisioning
- Billing — invoices, payment methods, and account management

For interactive testing, see the [API documentation](/api-docs/).

# Authentication

Most endpoints require authentication. Two methods are supported:

## API Key (Preferred)

Generate an API key from the [Account Security](https://my.interserver.net/account_security) page on [my.interserver.net](https://my.interserver.net/). Pass it in the `X-API-KEY` request header:

```
X-API-KEY: your-api-key-here
```

## Session-Based Authentication

Alternatively, authenticate by creating a session:

1. **Log in** — Send a `POST` request to `/login` with your account credentials. The response includes a session identifier.
2. **Pass the session ID** — Include the session identifier in the `sessionid` header on subsequent requests:

```
sessionid: your-session-id-here
```

API key authentication is recommended for most integrations as it does not expire and avoids the overhead of session management.

tags.name: Public
tags.description: Commands usable without an active session or api credentials.

/account.description:
This endpoint allows you to manage various user details, including contact information and account security.

- Use the `GET` method to retrieve your account information.
- Use the `POST` method to update your stored contact and billing information.

get.description: Returns the full account profile including contact information, billing address, and security settings. Use this to populate account management forms or verify account state before making changes with `POST /account`.

200.description: Your account information.

UpdateAccountInfo.description: Use the returned account data to pre-fill an update form and submit changes via POST /account.

post.description: Updates the stored contact and billing information on your account. Submit only the fields you want to change. Validation errors are returned as a 422 response with field-level messages.

422.description: Validation error while updating account data.

get.description: Returns the TOTP secret key needed to configure a two-factor authentication app (e.g. Google Authenticator). Present the `2fa_google_key` as a QR code or display the `2fa_google_split` value for manual entry. After setup, verify with `POST /account/2fa`.

200.description: Two-factor authentication setup data.

post.description: Verifies the TOTP code from your authenticator app and enables two-factor authentication on the account. Use `GET /account/2fa` first to retrieve the secret key for app setup.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

422.description: Provided two-factor verification code was invalid.

delete.description: Disables two-factor authentication on the account. After disabling, the account will only require password-based authentication.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Generates a new API key for the account. The previous key is immediately invalidated. Store the new key securely as it cannot be retrieved later.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Adds a new credit card to the account for billing. Card details are validated and stored securely. The card may require verification before it can be used as a payment method.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Updates an existing credit card on the account. Use this to refresh stored card metadata such as expiration date or billing address.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID. Use the card ID returned from `POST /account/creditcards` or listed in `/billing/creditcards`.

delete.description: Removes a credit card from the account. If this is the default payment method, select a new default via `/billing/payment_method` afterward.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID. Use the card ID returned from `POST /account/creditcards` or listed in `/billing/creditcards`.

post.description: Updates account-level feature flags that control service capabilities. These flags can disable password reset, OS reinstall, or other potentially destructive operations across your services. Changes take effect immediately.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

422.description: Request was valid but did not contain updatable values.

post.description: Adds an IP address range to the account's access restriction list. Once IP limiting is active, only requests originating from allowed ranges can access the account. Provide the start and end of the range in dotted-quad notation.

requestBody.description: The lower and upper bounds of an ip range.

422.description: IP limit payload contains an invalid address.

patch.description: Removes an IP address range from the account's access restriction list. If this is the last range, IP limiting is effectively disabled and the account becomes accessible from any IP address.

200.description: IP Range removed.

delete.description: Unlinks a third-party OAuth provider from the account. After unlinking, that provider can no longer be used for login.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Logs out of the specified OAuth provider session.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Changes the account login password. The current password must be provided for verification. After a successful change, existing API keys remain valid but active sessions may require re-authentication.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

post.description: Updates the SSH public key stored on the account. This key can be automatically installed on new VPS and server orders.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Changes the login username for the account. The new username must be unique across all accounts. After changing, use the new username for all future logins.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

400.description: The specified resource was not found

get.description: Returns the catalog of available affiliate banner images with their dimensions. Use these assets to build marketing creatives for your affiliate campaigns. Each banner includes the image filename, width, and height for layout purposes.

200.description: Affiliate Banners Array

post.description: Updates the affiliate landing page configuration, including the title, description, and coupon code. Visitors who arrive through your affiliate link see this customized page. Changes are published immediately.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

post.description: Updates the affiliate dock settings including the referral coupon and marketing copy. The dock is the branded landing page shown to visitors arriving via your affiliate link. Use this to customize the coupon code and promotional text.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

post.description: Updates how you receive affiliate commission payouts. Choose between prepay credit applied to your account balance or PayPal disbursement. When selecting PayPal, provide the email address linked to your PayPal account.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

get.description: Returns a detailed affiliate performance report with commission totals, conversion rates, and traffic summary. Use this for a comprehensive overview of your affiliate program performance in a single request.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

get.description: Returns time-series sales data for the requested number of days. Use this to render sales trend charts in an affiliate dashboard. Each data point represents aggregated sales for a time period.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days of sales history to include in the graph data. Determines the time window for the returned data points.

200.description: Affiliate sales graph data broken down by time period.

get.description: Returns the affiliate sales report with commission amounts and order summaries. Use this for tabular sales data export or to reconcile commission payouts against individual referral orders.

200.description: Response with a text message field.

get.description: Returns time-series traffic data for the requested number of days. Use this to render click and visit trend charts in an affiliate dashboard. Each data point represents aggregated traffic counts for a time period.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Number of days of traffic history to include in the graph data. Determines the time window for the returned data points.

200.description: Affiliate traffic graph data broken down by time period.

get.description: Returns individual web traffic log entries for affiliate referrals, including visitor IP address, referral URL, and timestamp. Use this to audit traffic sources, identify top referrers, or investigate suspicious click patterns.

200.description: The recent affiliate web traffic

get.description: Returns all backup storage services on your account. Each entry includes the `backup_id`, `backup_username`, `backup_ip`, `backup_status`, and `backup_quota`. Use the `backup_id` with `/backups/{id}` to retrieve full service details or `/backups/{id}/login` to obtain a storage panel session.

200.description: The listing of backup storage services on your account.

GetBackupDetails.description: Use the backup_id from the list to retrieve full service details including connection info and status.

get.description: Returns available backup storage plans, pricing tiers, and form metadata needed to build an order form. Use the service type IDs from this response when submitting a validation request via `PUT /backups/order` or placing an order via `POST /backups/order`.

200.description: Information needed to generate an order form.

put.description: Validates a backup storage order without placing it, returning calculated pricing and any validation errors. Use this to display a confirmation screen with the final price before submitting the order via `POST /backups/order`.

200.description: Validate Backup Order Response

post.description: Places a new backup storage order and generates an invoice. On success, the response includes invoice IDs that can be used with `/billing/invoices/{id}` to view the invoice or `/pay/{method}/{invoices}` to complete payment. The service is provisioned after payment is confirmed.

200.description: Response from the backup order call including invoice IDs for payment.

InitiatePayment.description: Use the invoice ID from the order response to retrieve invoice details or proceed to payment.

get.description: Returns detailed service information for the specified backup storage order, including `backup_username`, `backup_ip`, `backup_status`, and `backup_quota` in `serviceInfo`. Also returns `client_links`, `billingDetails`, `extraInfoTables`, `package`, and `custCurrency`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

200.description: Full backup service details including serviceInfo, billingDetails, and client_links.

GetBackupInvoices.description: Retrieve invoices for this backup service.

GetBackupLogin.description: Obtain a login session URL for the backup storage panel.

post.description: Updates backup storage service metadata, such as stored credentials or settings for the order.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

delete.description: Cancels the specified backup storage service. The service remains accessible until the end of the current billing period. This action cannot be undone; a new order must be placed to restore service.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

200.description: Backups Cancel

get.description: Retrieves invoices associated with the backup storage order. Use this to confirm billing status or locate invoices for payment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

200.description: Get Invoices response

get.description: Creates and returns a login session URL for the backup storage panel. The returned session URL can be used to redirect the user directly into the storage management interface without requiring separate credentials.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

200.description: Login session details for the backup storage service.

get.description: Resends the welcome email for the specified backup service. The email contains connection credentials and setup instructions. Use this when the original welcome email was lost or never received.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The backup service ID. Use the `backup_id` from `GET /backups` to identify the service.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns the current cart contents, available payment methods, and checkout metadata for the authenticated account. Use this to display the cart page, show totals, and determine which payment options are available before directing the user to `/pay/{method}/{invoices}`.
Lists prepay balances and their associated metadata. Use this to determine whether an account has usable prepay funds before selecting `prepay` as a payment method.

post.description: Creates a new prepay balance deposit and returns the invoice ID that must be paid to activate it. The prepay balance can then be used as a payment method for future orders. Use `/billing/invoices/{id}` to retrieve the generated invoice details.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

delete.description: Deletes a prepay balance from the account. The balance must have remaining funds to be eligible for deletion. Use `GET /billing/prepays` to list available prepay balances and their IDs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The prepay balance ID to delete.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns the invoice list for the account with summary totals. Use the invoice IDs from the response with `/billing/invoices/{id}` to retrieve detailed line items, or with `/pay/{method}/{invoices}` to initiate payment.

200.description: Invoice listing and summary for the account.

GetInvoiceDetails.description: Use an invoice ID from the list to retrieve the full invoice details for payment or review.

get.description: Retrieves the full invoice information including line items, amounts, and payment status. Use this before redirecting to `/pay/{method}/{invoices}` so you can display the exact amount due and confirm the invoice is still unpaid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The invoice ID. Use IDs from `GET /billing/invoices` or from order responses.

200.description: Detailed invoice payload for the requested invoice.

DeleteInvoice.description: Delete this invoice if it is still unpaid.

delete.description: Deletes a pending (unpaid) invoice from the account. Only invoices with a pending payment status can be deleted. Paid invoices cannot be removed. Related service records and repeat invoices are also cleaned up.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The invoice ID to delete. Only unpaid invoices can be deleted.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Adds a new credit card to the account for use as a payment method. If the card requires verification, the response indicates the next step. Complete verification via `GET /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify` followed by `POST /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify` before the card can be used for payments.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Updates stored credit card metadata or retrieves the masked card details. Use this to refresh card data before verification or to update billing address information associated with the card.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID. Use IDs from `GET /billing/creditcards` or the response from `POST /billing/creditcards`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

delete.description: Removes the selected credit card from the account. Use `/billing/payment_method` to select a new default payment method after deleting a card.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID to remove. Use IDs from `GET /billing/creditcards`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Retrieves the verification requirements for a newly added credit card. The response indicates whether the card requires micro-charge amount confirmation or CVV validation. Use this before presenting a verification form to the user.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID to verify. Use the ID returned from `POST /billing/creditcards`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Completes the credit card verification flow by submitting the micro-charge amounts or CVV as required by `GET /billing/creditcards/{id}/verify`. A successful response means the card is verified and can be selected as a payment method via `/billing/payment_method`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The credit card ID to verify. Use the ID returned from `POST /billing/creditcards`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Updates the account's default payment method, including selecting a verified credit card as the primary payment source or switching to PayPal when available.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns a base64-encoded JPEG captcha image for human verification. Display this image to the user and submit their response along with the registration or order form. No authentication is required for this endpoint.

200.description: An array containing a `captcha` field containing a string with a base64 encoded captcha image.

get.description: Returns the list of available Rapid Deploy dedicated servers with current pricing. Each entry includes CPU, memory, disk, bandwidth, IP allocation, and location details. These servers are pre-configured and can be provisioned immediately after purchase.

200.description: Marketplace Buy it now servers list

/dns.description: View and manage your DNS domains and records

get.description: Returns the DNS domains on your account along with their primary A record content. Use the `id` from each entry with `/dns/{id}` to retrieve the full record set, or to add, update, and delete individual records.

200.description: Listing of DNS domains on the account with their primary A record.

GetDnsDomain.description: Use the domain id from the list to retrieve all DNS records for that domain.

post.description: Creates a new DNS domain and assigns an initial A record pointing to the supplied IP address. The domain is immediately available on InterServer's DNS servers. Use `/dns/{id}` to manage records after creation.

GetNewDnsDomain.description: After creating the domain, use the returned domain ID with GET /dns/{id} to view its records.

/dns/{id}.description: View and manage a domain and the records for it.

get.description: Returns the full set of DNS records for the specified domain, including NS, A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, and other record types. Use the record `id` values with `/dns/{domainId}/{recordId}` to update or delete individual records.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The DNS domain ID. Use the `id` from `GET /dns` to identify the domain.

200.description: The DNS records for the specified domain.

UpdateDnsRecord.description: Use a record id from the list to update that DNS record.

DeleteDnsRecord.description: Use a record id from the list to delete that DNS record.

post.description: Adds a new DNS record to the specified domain. Provide the record type (A, AAAA, CNAME, MX, TXT, etc.), name, content, TTL, and priority. The record takes effect on the DNS servers immediately. Use `GET /dns/{id}` afterward to confirm the record was created.

200.description: Add DNS Domain Response

delete.description: Deletes a DNS domain and all of its associated records from the DNS servers. This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Any services relying on these DNS records will be affected immediately.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The DNS domain ID to delete. Use the `id` from `GET /dns` to identify the domain.

/dns/{domainId}/{recordId}.description: Manage a DNS Record for the given Domain.

post.description: Updates an existing DNS record with new values. Use `GET /dns/{id}` to list records and retrieve the record IDs before updating. Changes propagate to the DNS servers immediately.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: The DNS domain ID. Use the `id` from `GET /dns` to identify the domain.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: The DNS record ID within the domain. Use the record `id` from `GET /dns/{id}` to identify the record.

requestBody.description: The request data to update a dns record.

delete.description: Removes a DNS record from the specified domain. The deletion takes effect on the DNS servers immediately. Use `GET /dns/{id}` to verify the record has been removed.

parameters.name: domainId
parameters.description: The DNS domain ID. Use the `id` from `GET /dns` to identify the domain.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: The DNS record ID within the domain. Use the record `id` from `GET /dns/{id}` to identify the record.

get.description: Returns the domain services on your account, including the billing order ID, hostname, expiration date, and status. Use the `domain_id` from this response with `GET /domains/{id}` to fetch full service details, or with `/domains/{id}/nameservers`, `/domains/{id}/contact`, and `/domains/{id}/whois` to manage registration settings.

200.description: The listing of `Domains` services on your account.

getDomainInfo.description: Use the `domain_id` from any item in the response array to fetch full service details via `GET /domains/{id}`.

get.description: Checks a domain for availability and premium status, and returns pricing information for new, renewal, and transfer actions when possible. The response also includes the order field metadata you can pass to `/domains/order` when placing a registration or transfer.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The full domain name to look up (for example `example.com`).

200.description: Availability, pricing, and field metadata for the requested domain.

addDomain.description: Use the pricing and field metadata from this lookup response to populate the order request for `POST /domains/order`.

get.description: Queries the registrar for suggested domains and availability data. Use the returned `lookup` results to identify availability and then call `/domains/lookup/{name}` or `/domains/order` to obtain pricing and order fields for the chosen domain.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The base domain name to search (for example `example` or `example.com`).

200.description: Suggested and lookup results for the supplied search term.

404.description: No search suggestions or registrar response available.

value.message: Invalid Search Response!  Either there was an error communicating with the registrars or We received no results.

get.description: Returns the catalog of available domain services, including TLD service IDs and base Whois privacy pricing. Use the `tldServices` map to resolve a TLD into a service ID when you build an order request for `/domains/order` (POST) or the domain-field lookup endpoints.

200.description: Domain registration order information.

put.description: Returns validation data for a domain order request (such as availability and pricing information). This is typically used as a pre-flight check before submitting a POST order.

200.description: Validate Domain Order response

post.description: Places a new domain registration or transfer order. Use the results from `/domains/lookup/{name}` or `/domains/order/{domain}/{regType}` to populate the required domain fields before submitting the order.

patch.description: Returns the domain-specific field requirements for the supplied hostname and registration type. Use this to build the form data required for `/domains/order` (POST).

200.description: Validate Domain order response

get.description: Returns order data for the specified domain hostname, including availability and pricing details. Use this to preview order information before placing a registration or transfer.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: The fully qualified domain name to look up (e.g. `example.com`).

200.description: Domain availability and pricing information.

get.description: Returns the registration-type-specific form fields required to place a domain order for the given hostname. Use the returned fields to build the POST body for `POST /domains/order`.

parameters.name: regType
parameters.description: The registration type. Common values include `register` for new registrations and `transfer` for inbound transfers.

200.description: Registration-type-specific form field definitions.

get.description: Fetches the detailed domain service record (billing, registration, and service metadata) for the specified order ID. Use the `domain_hostname` from this response when calling `/domains/lookup/{name}` or when interacting with nameserver and DNSSEC management endpoints.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: Domain Information.

getDomainContact.description: Retrieve the registrant and admin contact details for this domain.

getDomainDnssec.description: Retrieve the DNSSEC DS records for this domain.

getDomainInvoices.description: Retrieve the billing invoices for this domain order.

getDomainWhoisPrivacy.description: Retrieve the Whois privacy status for this domain.

post.description: Updates the domain service record for the order. Use this for account-level changes such as updating stored registration metadata or transfer attributes.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

delete.description: Cancels the domain order in the billing system. After cancellation the service will no longer auto-renew and will transition to a `canceled` status. The domain registration itself may remain active at the registrar until its current expiration date, but no further renewals will be processed. Treat the domain as inactive in downstream automation after this call succeeds.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: Domains Cancel

get.description: Returns the current domain contact details that will be used for the registrant, admin, technical, and billing contact records. Use this response as the basis for any contact updates.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: The registrant/admin contact details for the domain.

post.description: Updates the registrant/admin contact details for the domain. Submit the same fields returned by the GET response after making the desired changes. Domain updates require the service to be active and unlocked.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns the DNSSEC DS records registered with the domain. Use the record data if you need to synchronize DNSSEC settings with another registry or to prepare an update request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: DNSSEC records currently applied to the domain.

post.description: Adds DNSSEC DS records to the domain registration. Provide one or more DNSSEC record entries (algorithm, digest type, digest, key tag). Registrations must be active.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

delete.description: Removes DNSSEC DS records from the domain registration. Use this to disable DNSSEC at the registrar level.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Set to `delete` to remove all DNSSEC records.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Retrieves invoice records associated with the domain order. Use this to reconcile billing or to confirm whether renewals or add-ons (such as Whois privacy) have been invoiced.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: Get Invoices response

get.description: Starts the domain renewal workflow for an active domain. This endpoint currently returns a status message indicating that renewal handling has been triggered.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Submits a domain renewal request for the order. Use `/domains/{id}/invoices` to identify any invoices created as part of the renewal flow.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Initiates the transfer workflow for a domain already in your account. Use this in coordination with `/domains/{id}/contact` to ensure registrant details are ready for the transfer request.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Submits a transfer request for the domain order. Use `/domains/{id}/invoices` to identify any invoices generated as part of the transfer process.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Resends the welcome email for the domain service. The email contains registration details and management instructions.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns status text for the Whois privacy add-on on the domain. Use this to determine whether privacy can be enabled or disabled for the current TLD before submitting updates.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

post.description: Enables or disables the Whois privacy add-on for the domain. The API may create invoices when enabling privacy; use `/domains/{id}/invoices` to track billing events.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The domain service ID. Use `domain_id` from `GET /domains`.

200.description: A response indicating the operation completed successfully with a text message.

get.description: Returns all Floating IP services on the account with their current status and assignment details.

200.description: The listing of `Floating IPs` services on your account.

getFloatingIpInfo.description: Use an ID from the response array to fetch full details via `GET /floating_ips/{id}`.

get.description: Retrieves available options and pricing for ordering a new Floating IP.

put.description: Validates a Floating IP order before placing it. Use this to check for errors before committing to a purchase.

200.description: Validate Floating IPs order response

post.description: Places an order for a new Floating IP service. Use `PUT /floating_ips/order` to validate the order first.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific Floating IP service including its current target IP assignment.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Floating IP service ID. Use the ID from `GET /floating_ips`.

post.description: Updates settings on a Floating IP service, such as its label or configuration metadata.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Floating IP service ID. Use the ID from `GET /floating_ips`.

delete.description: Cancels a Floating IP service. After cancellation the IP assignment is released and the service transitions to a canceled status. No further billing charges will be incurred.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Floating IP service ID. Use the ID from `GET /floating_ips`.

200.description: Floating Ip Cancel

post.description: Changes the target IP address that the Floating IP points to. The Floating IP service must be active. Use `GET /floating_ips/{id}` to view the current target before making changes.

parameters.name: id

apiKeyAuth.description:
In order to interact with the InterServer API you will need to first authenticate.  We support a number of different authentication methods.

To authenticate with the API Key you must get a key from your [Account Security](https://my.interserver.net/account_settings) page.

To use this key pass it in a header called `X-API-KEY`.

```
curl -H "X-API-KEY: $YOURE_KEY" "https://my.interserver.net/apiv2/$CALL"
````
apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-KEY

sessionIdHeaderAuth.description:
In order to interact with the InterServer API you will need to first authenticate.  We support a number of different authentication methods.

To authenticate with the Session ID in a HTTP header you need to first get the Session ID from a Login request.

To use this key pass it in a header called `sessionid`.

```
curl -H "seessionid: $YOUR_SESSION_ID" "https://my.interserver.net/apiv2/$CALL"
````
sessionIdHeaderAuth.name: sessionid

sessionIdCookieAuth.description:
In order to interact with the InterServer API you will need to first authenticate.  We support a number of different authentication methods.

To authenticate with the Session ID in a Cookie you need to first get the Session ID from a Login request.

To use this key pass it in a cookie called `sessionid`.

```
curl -b "seessionid: $YOUR_SESSION_ID" "https://my.interserver.net/apiv2/$CALL"
````
sessionIdCookieAuth.name: sessionid
